evals.prompt: How would we know if our pipeline stopped working? Right now we just check the logs manually every morning.
evals.expected_output: Identify critical observability gap (MTTD = hours), recommend specific golden signal alerts on job failure rate and job count, reference SLO targets.
expectations: The response identifies manual morning checks as a critical observability gap
The response quantifies current MTTD as hours vs target of less than 5 minutes
The response recommends specific alert rules on job failure rate and job count drop

evals.prompt: Our pipeline failed 3 times this week. Each time I just restarted it. Is that okay?
evals.expected_output: Identify manual restarts as toil, calculate error budget burn, recommend Error Recovery Agent automation, push for post-mortem.
expectations: The response identifies manual restarts as toil
The response calculates or references error budget burn rate
The response recommends automating retries via the Error Recovery Agent
The response requires a post-mortem on root cause

evals.prompt: Should we ship the new publisher platform feature or fix the ElevenLabs fallback first?
evals.expected_output: Apply error budget policy: check remaining budget, ship feature if >50%, fix reliability if <25%, frame ElevenLabs as single point of failure.
expectations: The response checks the error budget before recommending either option
The response recommends shipping feature if error budget >50%, fixing reliability if <25%
The response identifies ElevenLabs as a single point of failure
The response explains that SLO error budget policy drives this decision
